campaigns.title: SVX — Webinar Re-engagement
campaigns.webinarAngle: Exclusivity — SVX investor audience gets early access to the Climate Tech Exit Lab framework before it goes public. BTC brings the content, SVX brings the list.
campaigns.ecosystemNote: SVX is a CAFIID member — same ecosystem as CAFIID. Useful context if Bryan wants to reference shared community.

flow.step2: Keyona sends draft to Bryan Duarte for greenlight

campaigns.waitingOn: Bryan Duarte's response to Keyona's proposed email to James. Draft sent to Bryan for approval — Bryan has not sent to James yet.
campaigns.usageNote: Draft proposed to Bryan Duarte. Once he approves, he sends to James at SVX.
campaigns.emailSubject: Picking back up — webinar idea for your community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey James,

Hope the new year is treating you well. We connected late last year and I've been thinking about a collaboration that could be a good fit for your community.

We're putting together a webinar series on climate tech exits — breaking down how exits actually work in this space for small-check investors who want to understand the landscape before they commit capital. It's practical, framework-driven content that honestly doesn't exist anywhere else right now.

I'd love to co-host one session with SVX. You bring the audience, we bring the content. Low lift on your end, real value for your community.

Worth a 20-minute call to see if it makes sense?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Ontario Clean Tech Industry Association — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Peer credibility — BTC as practitioners who understand the founder and operator journey. Frame as a real conversation, not a pitch.

flow.step2: Keyona sends draft to Bryan Duarte for greenlight

campaigns.usageNote: Draft ready. Keyona to send to Bryan Duarte for greenlight. Confirm contact name with Bryan first.
campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar collab idea — climate tech exits
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey [Name],

I'm Bryan — I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC.

I've been putting together a webinar series on how climate tech exits actually work, and I think your community would be a natural fit for a session. One co-hosted event — you bring the audience, we bring the content.

Worth a quick call to see if it makes sense?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Clean Tech North — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Regional community investment — BTC is building with the Canadian ecosystem. One co-hosted session as a proof point.

flow.step2: Keyona sends draft to Bryan Duarte for greenlight
flow.step3: Bryan Duarte sends to Bryan Watson

campaigns.waitingOn: Bryan Duarte's greenlight on the draft Keyona wrote for Bryan Watson.
campaigns.usageNote: Contact is Bryan Watson. Draft sent to Bryan Duarte for sign-off. Once approved, Bryan Duarte sends.
campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar collab — want to co-host one session?
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Bryan,

Hope you're feeling rested after the long weekend!

Bryan Duarte and I have been talking about doing more collaborative content this year, and he mentioned you and Clean Tech North. We think there are some natural synergies there.

We're launching the Climate Tech Exit Lab this year — an initiative for both founders and investors on what goes into successful climate tech investments and exits. We'd love to co-host one session with Clean Tech North to bring that content to your community.

Would you be open to a quick conversation to see if it's a fit?

Bryan

campaigns.title: CAFIID — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.contactOrgFull: Canada Forum for Impact Investment and Development
campaigns.webinarAngle: Ecosystem fit — CAFIID runs a Climate Finance Community of Practice and already hosts webinars. BTC's Exit Lab content is a natural extension of what their members are already engaging with. One co-hosted session drops into an audience that already cares.
campaigns.ecosystemNote: CAFIID has a Climate Finance CoP with its own webinar series — e.g. 'Scaling biochar and carbon credits' April 2026. SVX is a CAFIID member. Strong alignment.

flow.step2: Keyona sends draft to Bryan Duarte for greenlight

campaigns.usageNote: Full org name confirmed: Canada Forum for Impact Investment and Development. Draft ready. Confirm contact name with Bryan Duarte, then Keyona sends for his greenlight.
campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar collab — climate tech exits for your community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey [Name],

I'm Bryan — I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC.

I've been following CAFIID's work and noticed your Climate Finance Community of Practice — the work your members are doing sits right at the intersection of what we're building. We're launching the Climate Tech Exit Lab this year, focused on how climate tech investments and exits actually work, and I think your community would find real value in a session on it.

I'd love to co-host one event with CAFIID. You bring the audience, we bring the content.

Worth a quick conversation?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Good Investor (Tim) — Webinar Pitch
campaigns.webinarAngle: Peer-to-peer — Tim's network is small-check investors trying to figure out the same things he is. One session gives them a real framework.

flow.step2: Keyona sends draft to Bryan Duarte for greenlight

Hey Tim,

Bryan here. Good to reconnect.

I'm putting together a webinar on how climate tech exits actually work — built for small-check investors who want a real framework before they commit capital. I'd love to co-host one session with you. You bring the people, we bring the content.

Interested?

Bryan
